This classification places Fagara mayu alongside other plants such as citrus fruits and their relatives. The genus Fagara is a group of evergreen shrubs and small trees, and Fagara mayu is one of the species within this group. Fagara mayu is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, where it can be found growing in the wild. The classification of Fagara mayu is used by botanists to identify and understand the relationships between different plant species.

intro: Fagara mayu, also known as Zanthoxylum mayu, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family and is native to the Andean region. It is valued for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Fagara mayu include limonoids, alkaloids, and flavonoids, such as mayuquinone and mayuridine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Fagara mayu is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions in the Andean region, and it is also used as a flavoring agent in local cuisine.
